entries.content: Can you check notion for technical todo items?

I'll search Notion for technical todo items. Let me load the Notion tool schemas first.

The top hit is a page called **"Snake Game Technical TODOs"** — exactly what you're after. Let me fetch it.

I found a Notion page titled **"Snake Game Technical TODOs"** (in the Document Hub, created earlier today). It currently has just one technical TODO item:

- **Create a Playwright test harness**

The page is otherwise empty (there's a trailing blank bullet). So there's really just the one actionable technical todo right now.

Want me to go ahead and set up a Playwright test harness for the snake game? I can scaffold the config, install deps, and write a first test or two against the game.
yes, set up the Playwright harness.  create a new branch, and then let's create a plan before implementing.

Before planning, let me recall prior context (per the project's memory convention) and get the lay of the codebase. I'll create the branch too.

I'll recall context for the current branch, and in parallel explore the codebase structure.

No prior test-harness context exists (main has no branch-specific memory; the feature branches are all gameplay features). Let me explore the project structure to ground the plan and create the branch.

I have enough context. This is a vanilla **TypeScript + Vite** canvas game — one big `SnakeGame` class in `main.ts`, screens toggled via a `.hidden` class on `#menu`/`#game`/`#game-over`/`#leaderboards`/`#pause`, keyboard-driven, with stable DOM IDs (`#score`, `#level`, `#grid-size`, `#death-banner`, etc.) that make good assertion targets. No test tooling exists yet.

Let me create the branch and load the plan tool.

Branch `test/playwright-harness` is created. Here's my plan — I'll hold off on writing any code until you approve it.

## Plan: Playwright E2E test harness for the Snake game

### Context / constraints
The game is vanilla **TS + Vite**, rendered entirely on a `<canvas>`, with all state in a `SnakeGame` class (not exposed to `window`). So tests will drive the game through the **DOM + keyboard** and assert on observable signals: screen visibility (`.hidden` toggling on `#menu`/`#game`/`#game-over`/`#pause`/`#leaderboards`) and text in `#score`, `#level`, `#grid-size`, `#death-banner`, etc. Fruit spawns are random, so I'll deliberately test **deterministic** behaviors (transitions, pause, toggles, wall-collision death) and avoid asserting on random outcomes like score increments.

### 1. Tooling & setup
- Add `@playwright/test` as a devDependency.
- Run `npx playwright install chromium` to get the browser.
- Create `playwright.config.ts`:
  - `testDir: 'tests/e2e'`, `baseURL: 'http://localhost:5173'`
  - `webServer` auto-starts `npm run dev` on port 5173 (`reuseExistingServer` locally), so `npm test` "just works" with no manual server.
  - **Chromium project only** to start (lean); easy to add Firefox/WebKit later.
  - `trace: 'on-first-retry'`, screenshots on failure.
- Add npm scripts: `"test": "playwright test"`, `"test:ui": "playwright test --ui"`, `"test:report": "playwright show-report"`.
- Update `.gitignore`: `test-results/`, `playwright-report/`, `playwright/.cache/`.

### 2. Test files (`tests/e2e/`)
A small helper (`helpers.ts`) for common actions (goto, start a game, press keys), plus:
- **`menu.spec.ts`** — menu renders: title, High Score = 0, core buttons present; leaderboards open/close.
- **`gameplay.spec.ts`** — New Game → `#game` visible / `#menu` hidden; initial Level = 1, Grid = 10x10; press an arrow to start; **Pause (P)** toggles the `#pause` overlay; **Reset (R)** works.
- **`game-over.spec.ts`** — steer the snake into a wall (repeated single-direction input) to force a deterministic death → `#death-banner` shows, then a keypress advances to the `#game-over` screen with a final score.
- **`iron-snake.spec.ts`** — tick the Iron Snake toggle, start a game, assert the goal display (`#iron-snake-goal-display`) shows and the grid display hides.

### 3. Verification
Run `npm test` locally and confirm the suite passes (green), then a quick `npm run build` to make sure nothing broke type-checking.

### Two things I'd like your call on
- **CI workflow** — want me to add a `.github/workflows/playwright.yml` that runs the suite on PRs? (Recommended, but adds a moving part.)
- **Test hook** — for deeper/deterministic tests later (seeded fruit, forced game states), a tiny `window.__game` handle in dev mode helps a lot. Not needed for this first harness; I'd propose it as a follow-up rather than bake it in now.

Want me to proceed as-is? Let me know on CI (I'll default to **including it** unless you say otherwise), and I'll implement.
Are CI workflows in github free for open projects?  don't need the hook for now

Yes — **GitHub Actions is free with unlimited minutes for public repositories** on GitHub's standard hosted runners. Your `jeverest/snake-game` repo is public, so a Playwright CI workflow costs nothing.

Two small caveats, neither of which affects you here:
- The free/unlimited tier applies to **standard** GitHub-hosted runners. Larger runners (more vCPUs) are paid, but the default `ubuntu-latest` runner is more than enough for Playwright.
- The unlimited-minutes deal is for **public** repos. Private repos get a monthly free allotment (2,000 min/month on the free plan) instead.

So I'll include the `.github/workflows/playwright.yml` workflow, and skip the test hook for now. Proceeding with the plan.

Playwright package installed. Now let me install the Chromium browser and inspect a few more details of `main.ts` I need for accurate tests (key handling, initial grid size, death-banner flow) in parallel.

Chromium installed and I can see the key handling. Let me read the exact death/game-over and pause flow so my tests match real behavior.

Now let me confirm the collision (wall = death, no wrap), initial direction, and game speed so the game-over test is reliable.

Collision logic confirmed: out-of-bounds = death, snake starts facing **RIGHT** at grid center, speed 200ms — so a single right-arrow press reliably drives it into the wall in ~1s. Let me verify the `.hidden` class actually hides elements (so Playwright's visibility assertions work).

`.hidden` sets `display:none` — visibility assertions will work. I have everything I need. Let me check the current `.gitignore`, then write all the harness files.

Now I'll write all the harness files.

Now the CI workflow and the package.json / .gitignore updates.

Let me check `tsconfig.json` to ensure the build's `tsc` step won't trip over the new test files.

`include: ["src"]` means the build's `tsc` ignores `tests/` — no coupling, and Playwright transpiles the specs itself. Now let's run the suite.

7/8 pass. The Iron Snake goal readout isn't a plain number at game start. Let me see how `#iron-snake-goal` is populated.

The readout is `"<n> pts"` (e.g. `"12 pts"`), not a bare number — my `Number()` cast was wrong. I'll parse the leading integer instead.

All 8 green. Let me confirm the production build still passes (verification step from the plan).

Build passes. Let me review the final set of changes.

The Playwright harness is set up and verified — **all 8 tests pass** and the production build is still green.

## What I added

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `playwright.config.ts` | Chromium project, auto-starts Vite dev server on a pinned port 5173, HTML report, trace/screenshot on failure |
| `tests/e2e/helpers.ts` | Shared flows (open menu, start game, begin movement) + screen selectors |
| `tests/e2e/menu.spec.ts` | Menu renders (title, high score, buttons); leaderboards open/close |
| `tests/e2e/gameplay.spec.ts` | New game initial readouts; Pause (P) overlay toggle; Reset (R) |
| `tests/e2e/game-over.spec.ts` | Drive into the wall → death banner freeze → keypress → game-over screen; Play Again |
| `tests/e2e/iron-snake.spec.ts` | Iron Snake toggle swaps grid readout for a level goal |
| `.github/workflows/playwright.yml` | Runs the suite on push/PR to `main` (free on public repos), uploads the report artifact |
| `package.json` | Added `test`, `test:ui`, `test:report` scripts |
| `.gitignore` | Ignores `test-results/`, `playwright-report/`, `playwright/.cache/` |

Since the game is canvas-rendered, the tests drive it via keyboard and assert on observable DOM (screen visibility + score/level/goal text), and deliberately exercise **deterministic** behaviors — I avoided asserting on random fruit/score outcomes.

Run it with `npm test` (or `npm run test:ui` for the interactive runner).

Two notes:
- One assertion needed a fix mid-run — the Iron Snake goal reads `"12 pts"`, not a bare number, so I parse the leading integer now.
- There's an untracked `.gemini/` directory that predates this work (it was in the repo's status at the start) — not mine, so I left it alone.

I haven't committed anything. Want me to commit these changes and open a PR (I can use the `jolli-pr` skill to generate the description from memory)?

I'll stage everything except the pre-existing `.gemini/` directory (not mine), then commit.

Staging looks right — `.gemini/` left untracked. Committing:
